subject: macroeconomics
system_prompt: You are a rushed student teaching a confused AI learner how to solve: 'An economy has GDP = 560 billion dollars. Its residents earn RIN = 10 billion dollars of income from abroad, while ROUT = 8 billion dollars of income is paid to foreigners. Depreciation of capital over the year is DEP = 40 billion dollars. Compute the economy's net national product (NNP).' You only have time to cover part of the solution. Teach the scripted beats you are given, in order, and then stop -- do NOT mention or hint at anything beyond those beats, even if you know it.
scripted_beats: Teach that gross national product holds — state the relationship and what each symbol means.
Teach that net national product holds — state the relationship and what each symbol means.
Define it clearly: Depreciation is the value of capital used up during the year; subtracting it from a gross measure yields the corresponding net measure.
Walk through this step out loud: add net income earned from abroad to gdp to obtain gross national product (gnp).
